title: populus nigra subsp. caudina
intro: Populus nigra subsp. caudina, also known as the Black Cottonwood or Balsam Poplar, is a subspecies of the Black Poplar tree native to North America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can be attributed to the presence of salicin, a compound similar to aspirin. The health benefits of Populus nigra subsp. caudina include relief from pain and fever, as well as potential treatments for skin conditions and wounds. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, or salves, and are often used in conjunction with other natural remedies to promote overall well-being.
